title: Overview
text:
This project provides a proof-of-concept stack to align user geometries against GRB reference data:

a FastAPI webservice (brdr_webservice.py)
a viewer (brdr-viewer) to draw/edit geometry and inspect BRDR steps
Docker and local scripts for quick startup

title: Overview
text:
This project provides a proof-of-concept stack to align user geometries against GRB reference data:

a FastAPI webservice (brdr_webservice.py)
a viewer (brdr-viewer) to draw/edit geometry and inspect BRDR steps
Docker and local scripts for quick startup

title: Overview
text:
What You Can Do

Start backend and viewer locally.
Draw or edit an input polygon in the viewer.
Trigger BRDR alignment.
Inspect intermediate steps on the map and in the chart timeline.

title: Overview
text:
Components at a Glance

Backend endpoint: POST /aligner (viewer-oriented response)
Frontend map + timeline: OpenLayers + React app in brdr-viewer/brdr-viewer

Continue with:

Architecture
Run Locally
API and Viewer

title: API and Viewer
API and Viewer
text:
Status Codes

200: Success
400: Validation or request-shape issue
503: Upstream GRB service unavailable
500: Unexpected internal error

title: API and Viewer
text:
GET /aligner
Returns a helper payload for end users with: - endpoint usage note - docs link - sample query/body

title: API and Viewer
text:
Viewer Integration
The viewer:

keeps an input geometry in local state
sends that geometry to /aligner
updates timeline and map layers from returned series and diffs
highlights prediction steps and can jump to previous/next prediction quickly

Main frontend files:

src/api/brdrApi.ts
src/state/useBrdrState.ts
src/components/map/MapView.tsx

Viewer routes:

/ overview page
/grb-viewer GRB demo viewer
/brk-viewer BRK/WFS demo viewer
/viewer redirects to /grb-viewer

title: API and Viewer
text:
Testing the API with curl
With body.json in repository root:
curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:80/aligner?feature_id=2" \
  -H "accept: application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @body.json

title: Architecture
text:
The viewer sends a GeoJSON feature collection to the backend.
The backend loads the configured reference data and evaluates multiple distances.
The backend returns viewer-oriented BRDR output via /aligner.
The viewer renders:

result geometry
result_diff_min (negative diff)
result_diff_plus (positive diff)
diffs chart values per distance step

title: Architecture
text:
The viewer sends a GeoJSON feature collection to the backend.
The backend loads the configured reference data and evaluates multiple distances.

result geometry

title: Architecture
text:
Backend

Framework: FastAPI
Main file: brdr_webservice.py
Typings/models: brdr_webservice_typings.py
BRDR dependency: brdr package

Important behavior:

Request validation on feature IDs (must be present and unique).
Compatibility handling for GRB type labels across BRDR versions.
503 when upstream GRB service is unavailable.

title: Architecture
text:
Viewer

Framework: React + TypeScript + Vite
Map library: OpenLayers
Path: brdr-viewer/brdr-viewer

Capabilities:

draw a new point/line/polygon
edit geometry vertices (where applicable)
auto-trigger recalculation after drawing/editing (manual recalc remains available)
inspect timeline steps, prediction markers, and geometry-aware diff chart

title: Architecture
text:
Runtime Modes

Native: Python backend + Node viewer
Containerized backend: Docker backend + Node viewer
One-command local startup: start-local.ps1 / start-local.cmd

title: Run Locally
text:
Python 3.x
Node.js 18+
npm
Optional: Docker Desktop

title: Run Locally
text:
Python 3.x
Node.js 18+
npm
Optional: Docker Desktop

title: Run Locally
text:
Fastest Start (Recommended)
From repository root:
.\start-local.ps1
Or:
start-local.cmd
This starts:

backend on http://127.0.0.1:80
viewer on http://127.0.0.1:5173
browser opens automatically

title: Run Locally
text:
Manual Start

1. Start backend
python brdr_webservice.py


2. Start viewer
cd brdr-viewer\brdr-viewer
npm install
npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173
Open http://127.0.0.1:5173.

title: Run Locally
text:
Docker Backend Option
From repository root:
docker build -f Dockerfile . -t brdr-aligner
docker run --rm -p 80:80 --name brdr-aligner brdr-aligner
Make sure Docker Desktop / Docker Engine is running before executing these commands.
This image bundles both backend and viewer:

API: http://127.0.0.1:80
Swagger: http://127.0.0.1:80/docs
Viewer: http://127.0.0.1:80/
BRK Viewer (WFS): http://127.0.0.1:80/brk-viewer

title: Run Locally
text:
Common Issues

Viewer shows fetch errors

Ensure backend is running on http://127.0.0.1:80.
Check CORS origin (localhost:5173 / 127.0.0.1:5173).
Verify outbound access from backend to GRB upstream services.



Empty or flat chart values

Trigger recalculation after drawing or editing geometry.
Ensure geometry is valid and has area.
